expectations.text: The review guide includes a Data Model Changes section
expectations.evidence: No header named 'Data Model Changes', 'Schema Changes', or 'Database Changes'. Uses '### Areas needing careful attention' with numbered subsections ('1. Destructive / irreversible data migration', '2. Breaking GraphQL schema change'). Neither subsection name qualifies as a Data Model category header per grading rules.

expectations.text: The review guide flags the RENAME COLUMN (username to display_name) or the GraphQL field rename as a potential breaking change for existing clients
expectations.evidence: '`RENAME COLUMN username TO display_name` — breaks any consumer still selecting `username`'; '`username: String!` → `displayName: String!` is a **breaking change** for every API client'

expectations.text: The review guide flags the DROP COLUMN or GraphQL field removal as a destructive/breaking operation
expectations.evidence: '`ALTER TABLE users DROP COLUMN legacy_id` — **data loss**'; '`legacyId: Int` removed — same concern; any client still requesting it will get a query validation error'

expectations.text: The review guide is wrapped in <!-- pr-human-guide --> and <!-- /pr-human-guide --> markers
expectations.evidence: No HTML comment markers in FINAL_PR_BODY; body uses '---' separator and plain '## Review Guide' heading. NOTES confirm no skill was invoked.

notes: Missing the required HTML wrapper markers (assertion 4) and the required 'Data Model Changes' (or equivalent) section header (assertion 1). Content quality for assertions 2 and 3 is excellent — very detailed flagging of both the rename and drop as breaking/destructive. Baseline (no-skill) run produced a free-form guide rather than the structured skill format.
